9.1.title: गुह्यतमम् — the superlative secrecy marker
9.1.body: Krishna opens with गुह्यतमम् — the तम-superlative of गुह्य, signalling that what follows outranks every earlier confidence. The relative-correlative यत् ज्ञात्वा ... मोक्ष्यसे binds knowing (ज्ञात्वा, क्त्वान्त absolutive) to liberation (मोक्ष्यसे, लृट् from √मुच्), making the chapter a single conditional promise.

9.1.title: अनसूयवे — addressing the non-cavilling listener
9.1.body: The dative अनसूयवे (चतुर्थी एकवचन of अनसूयु) is precisely targeted: the teaching is given because Arjuna does not cavil. विज्ञानसहितम् pairs ज्ञान with विज्ञान — knowledge with its realised application — flagging that this chapter is not just doctrine but pratyakṣa-grade insight.

9.2.title: Four superlatives stack on one इदम्
9.2.body: राजविद्या, राजगुह्यम्, पवित्रम्, उत्तमम् — four predicate nominatives all in सामानाधिकरण्य with इदम्, piling regal vocabulary onto the teaching. राज- as a compound prefix means 'king of', so the verse declares this knowledge sovereign among knowledges and supreme among secrets simultaneously.

9.2.title: प्रत्यक्षावगमम् — verifiable, not creedal
9.2.body: The adjective प्रत्यक्षावगमम् (whose comprehension is direct/perceptual) is Krishna's epistemic move: this is not śabda-pramāṇa alone but verifiable in experience. सुसुखं कर्तुम् uses an infinitive of purpose with सुसुख — 'easy to perform' — countering the assumption that the highest knowledge must be the hardest practice.

9.3.title: अश्रद्दधानाः — the faithless return
9.3.body: अश्रद्दधानाः is a present participle of श्रद्-धा with negating अ-, naming those who refuse to trust the teaching. अप्राप्य (क्त्वान्त absolutive of आ-√आप्) plus निवर्तन्ते (लट्) gives the bleak sequence: not having reached Me, they turn back — onto मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि, the locative 'on the road of death-and-saṃsāra'.

9.3.title: परन्तप — a vocative that sets the contrast
9.3.body: Krishna addresses Arjuna as परन्तप ('scorcher of foes') precisely when describing those who lack tapas-of-faith — the epithet is a rhetorical foil, calling Arjuna toward what the अश्रद्दधानाः refuse. मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि is a long तत्पुरुष locative, the very road Arjuna's despondency in chapter 1 was beginning to walk.

9.4.title: मया ततम् — instrumental of pervasion
9.4.body: मया is तृतीया एकवचन — 'by Me' — and ततम् (past passive participle of √तन्) means 'stretched/pervaded'. The instrumental + ppp construction gives a passive perfect: the whole world stands already pervaded by an अव्यक्त-मूर्ति, an unmanifest form. मत्स्थानि is a बहुव्रीहि — 'whose abode is in Me'.

9.4.title: The paradox: न च अहं तेषु अवस्थितः
9.4.body: Krishna asserts both मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि (beings rest in Me) and न च अहं तेषु अवस्थितः (yet I do not rest in them). The locative तेषु is the pivot — containment is one-directional. This is the chapter's metaphysical core: pervasion without reciprocal dependence, which 9.5 will call योगम् ऐश्वरम्.

9.5.title: योगम् ऐश्वरम् — the lordly paradox-power
9.5.body: Having just said मत्स्थानि भूतानि, Krishna reverses with न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि and labels the contradiction itself योगम् ऐश्वरम् — sovereign yoga. भूतभृत् (sustainer of beings) and न भूतस्थः (not located in beings) stand side by side as nominative tatpuruṣas, refusing logical resolution.

9.5.title: ममात्मा भूतभावनः — agent without residence
9.5.body: The compound भूतभावनः (one who brings beings into being) names a causal role; ममात्मा keeps it possessive — 'My Self is the bringer-forth'. The verse withholds spatial categories: pervasion, sustenance, and origination are affirmed while location is denied. पश्य (लोट् of √दृश्) makes it a thing to see, not infer.

9.6.title: आकाश-वायु — the analogy of containment
9.6.body: The यथा–तथा correlative carries the argument: as वायु though सर्वत्रगः (going everywhere) remains आकाशस्थितः (stationed in ākāśa), so all beings are मत्स्थानि. The locative आकाशस्थितः and the nominative महान् together hint that even the greatest mobility presupposes a still ground.

9.6.title: उपधारय — the imperative of intellectual grasp
9.6.body: उपधारय is लोट् मध्यम-पुरुष of उप-√धृ — 'firmly hold this in mind'. Krishna does not say 'see' or 'know' but 'sustain-this-conclusion', because the analogy is to be carried, not merely perceived. इति marks the preceding clause as the content of that mental holding.

9.7.title: Cosmic rhythm in two लट् verbs
9.7.body: Two present-tense verbs frame cosmic time: यान्ति (beings 'go' to My प्रकृति at कल्पक्षये, locative of dissolution) and विसृजामि (I 'emit' them at कल्पादौ, locative of beginning). The locatives of time anchor the cycle; the लट् keeps it ever-present, not past mythology.

9.7.title: मामिकाम् — the possessive feminine
9.7.body: मामिकाम् is a rare possessive adjective ('belonging to Me') agreeing with प्रकृतिम् — द्वितीया एकवचन स्त्रीलिंग. The form insists that prakṛti is not autonomous; it is Krishna's. The vocative कौन्तेय softens what would otherwise sound like a dispassionate cosmological report.

9.8.title: अवष्टभ्य — leaning on one's own nature
9.8.body: अवष्टभ्य is a ल्यबन्त absolutive of अव-√स्तम्भ् (-य allomorph after prefix, per Pāṇini 7.1.37) ('having taken hold of, leaning upon'). Krishna 'leans on' His own प्रकृति to emit the entire भूतग्रामम् — a collective noun for the whole creature-aggregate. The repetition पुनः पुनः stresses recurrence, not a single creation event.

9.8.title: अवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् — helplessness under nature's sway
9.8.body: अवशम् (helpless) modifying भूतग्रामम्, paired with the अब्लेटिव-of-cause प्रकृतेर्वशात् ('from the sway of prakṛti'), confesses that beings are emitted without consent. The word-play वश–अवश makes the bondage audible: subject to vaśa precisely by being avaśa.

9.9.title: उदासीनवत् आसीनम् — the simile of detached sitting
9.9.body: The taddhita -वत् in उदासीनवत् creates a simile adverb: 'as one indifferent'. Paired with the participle आसीनम् (seated), it pictures Krishna as a witness presiding rather than acting. असक्तम् तेषु कर्मसु — locative plural — denies attachment specifically to those very acts of cosmic emission.

9.9.title: Why karma does not bind the actor
9.9.body: न निबध्नन्ति is लट् with the negation, agreeing with कर्माणि as subject and मां as object: the acts do not bind. The reason is given by उदासीन-वत् — the asaṅga stance dissolves the karmic tie. Arjuna, addressed as धनञ्जय, is being shown the model for his own कर्मयोग.

9.10.title: मया अध्यक्षेण — the instrumental of supervision
9.10.body: मया अध्यक्षेण is तृतीया एकवचन — 'with Me as overseer'. प्रकृतिः सूयते (लट् ātmanepada of √सू, 'gives birth') makes prakṛti the proximate agent and Krishna the supervising condition. सचराचरम् ('with moving and unmoving') is an adverbial accusative qualifying what prakṛti produces.

9.10.title: हेतुना अनेन — the causal instrumental
9.10.body: हेतुना अनेन ('by this cause') refers back to the supervisory presence, and विपरिवर्तते (लट् of वि-परि-√वृत्) describes the world 'revolving on all sides'. The verse offers a two-tier causality: prakṛti as material producer, Krishna's अध्यक्षत्व as the cause of the producing.

9.11.title: अवजानन्ति — the verb of contempt
9.11.body: अवजानन्ति (लट् of अव-√ज्ञा, 'to look down upon') governs मां and is the chapter's clinical name for the moron's reception. The participle आश्रितम् ('having taken on') with मानुषीं तनुम् in द्वितीया explains the trigger: the human body is mistaken for the whole reality.

9.11.title: भूतमहेश्वरम् — the अजानन्तः miss this
9.11.body: अजानन्तः is the negated present participle of √ज्ञा, agreeing with मूढाः: 'not knowing'. What they miss is परं भावम् … भूतमहेश्वरम् — 'the supreme mode … the great lord of beings'. The contempt and the ignorance are grammatically yoked: one participle (अजानन्तो) supplies the cause of the other verb (अवजानन्ति).

9.12.title: मोघ-त्रयम् — three futilities in apposition
9.12.body: मोघाशाः, मोघकर्माणः, मोघज्ञानाः stand as three predicate nominatives, each a बहुव्रीहि compound with मोघ ('vain'): vain-hope, vain-action, vain-knowledge. विचेतसः ('whose intelligence is scattered') sums them up. The verse is structurally a cascade — at every cognitive layer, the मूढ misfires.

9.12.title: मोहिनीम् प्रकृतिम् — the deluding nature taken as refuge
9.12.body: श्रिताः (past passive participle of √श्रि, 'having taken refuge in') governs the द्वितीया प्रकृतिम्, qualified by राक्षसीम् आसुरीम् मोहिनीम् — three feminine adjectives in सामानाधिकरण्य. The very faculty that should liberate (आश्रय) has been pointed at the deluding mode, which is why every output is मोघ.

9.13.title: दैवीं प्रकृतिम् आश्रिताः — the counter-refuge
9.13.body: Mirroring 9.12, the महात्मानः take refuge in दैवीं प्रकृतिम् — the divine mode of nature, द्वितीया एकवचन. आश्रिताः again is the ppp of √श्रि, now positively oriented. The same act (आश्रय) saves or damns depending on which प्रकृति is its object.

9.13.title: अनन्यमनसः — the बहुव्रीहि of single-minded devotion
9.13.body: अनन्यमनसः is a बहुव्रीहि — 'those whose minds have no other (object)'. The participle ज्ञात्वा (absolutive of √ज्ञा) governs भूतादिम् अव्ययम् — 'the beginning of beings, the imperishable'. भजन्ति is लट् with मां: knowing first, then worshipping. ज्ञान precedes भक्ति here.

9.14.title: Four participles, one verb
9.14.body: कीर्तयन्तः, यतन्तः, नमस्यन्तः — three present participles in प्रथमा बहुवचन — orbit the single finite verb उपासते (लट् ātmanepada of उप-√आस्). सततम् as adverb governs the first; भक्त्या is तृतीया ('with devotion') governing the third. The grammar enacts what it describes: many modes, one practice.

9.14.title: दृढव्रताः, नित्ययुक्ताः — predicate adjectives of constancy
9.14.body: दृढव्रताः ('whose vow is firm', बहुव्रीहि) and नित्ययुक्ताः ('ever-yoked') are nominative-plural predicates of the same devotees. The repetition of nitya-/satatam-/dṛḍha- vocabulary is deliberate: where 9.13 emphasised knowing, 9.14 emphasises persistence.

9.15.title: ज्ञानयज्ञेन — the instrumental of cognitive worship
9.15.body: ज्ञानयज्ञेन is तृतीया एकवचन — 'by the sacrifice that is knowledge' — naming a third path alongside the कीर्तन of 9.14. यजन्तः (present participle of √यज्) and उपासते (लट् of उप-√आस्) layer the verbal act: sacrificing-and-worshipping as one continuous mode.

9.15.title: एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम्
9.15.body: Three instrumentals (एकत्वेन, पृथक्त्वेन, बहुधा-via taddhita) catalogue three approaches: as One, as distinct, as manifold. विश्वतोमुखम् ('whose face is on every side') is the द्वितीया object of worship — Krishna in His omnidirectional aspect. The verse legitimises plural philosophical methods toward one referent.

9.16.title: अहम् — the predicate pronoun repeated seven times
9.16.body: Krishna identifies Himself with seven ritual elements — क्रतुः, यज्ञः, स्वधा, औषधम्, मन्त्रः, आज्यम्, अग्निः, हुतम् — by simple nominative-equation: 'I am X'. There is no copula; the bare prathama-prathama juxtaposition is the grammar of identity. एव intensifies one of them, marking the recitative rhythm.

9.16.title: Vedic and laukika items collapse into one referent
9.16.body: क्रतुः and यज्ञः are Vedic sacrifice-categories; स्वधा is the pitr offering; औषधम् is herb/medicine; मन्त्रः is the recited formula; आज्यम् the clarified butter; अग्निः the fire; हुतम् the oblation itself. Krishna identifies as cause (mantra), instrument (ghee, fire), and result (huta) — the entire sacrificial chain reduces to one nominative referent.

9.17.title: Kinship and Veda in one nominative cascade
9.17.body: पिता, माता, धाता, पितामहः name four kinship-roles of Krishna toward अस्य जगतः (षष्ठी, 'of this world'). The verse then shifts register: वेद्यम्, पवित्रम्, ओङ्कारः, ऋक्, साम, यजुः — the very objects of Vedic knowledge. The grammar is again bare prathama-equation, no verb.

9.17.title: ओङ्कारः and the three Vedas
9.17.body: ओङ्कारः is identified before ऋक्साम-यजुः, because the syllable ॐ logically precedes the three Vedas it heads. एव च at the end is emphatic-inclusive, sealing the list. The verse joins the conjugal–kinship metaphor (mother, father, grandfather) to the scriptural canon — both are Krishna.

9.18.title: Ten nominatives without a single verb
9.18.body: गतिः, भर्ता, प्रभुः, साक्षी, निवासः, शरणम्, सुहृत्, प्रभवः, प्रलयः, स्थानम्, निधानम्, बीजम् — a verbless cascade of predicate nominatives. The implied copula 'अस्मि' (or 'अहम् अस्मि') is supplied from 9.16-17. अव्ययम् ('imperishable') closes the list as a single qualifying adjective.

9.18.title: प्रभव-प्रलय paired — origin and end
9.18.body: प्रभवः and प्रलयः stand together — Krishna is both the arising and the dissolution. स्थानम् ('abode') and निधानम् ('treasure-house') frame the middle term. बीजम् अव्ययम् ('imperishable seed') is the verse's last word — the cause that itself never decays, completing the self-identification triad of 9.16-18.

9.19.title: Three लट् verbs of cosmic regulation
9.19.body: तपामि (I heat), निगृह्णामि (I hold back), उत्सृजामि (I release) — three present-tense verbs in उत्तम-पुरुष एकवचन. वर्षम् is the द्वितीया object of both नि-√ग्रह् and उत्-√सृज्. Krishna is the regulating agent of sun-heat and rain-cycle, articulated as continuous action, not myth.

9.19.title: अमृतं ... मृत्युश्च, सद् असच्च — the polar pairs
9.19.body: Two coordinated pairs — अमृत/मृत्यु and सत्/असत् — are claimed in a single nominative-equation with अहम्. च ... च ('both ... and') signals deliberate inclusion of both poles. This sets up the metaphysical width that 9.20-21 will then narrow to the Vedic ritualists who grasp only one side.

9.20.title: त्रैविद्याः and the meter shift to त्रिष्टुप्
9.20.body: The verse shifts to a longer meter (त्रिष्टुप् family) signalling a register change. त्रैविद्याः ('knowers of the three Vedas') and सोमपाः ('soma-drinkers') are nominative plural compounds. पूतपापाः is a बहुव्रीहि — 'whose sins have been purified'. The participle इष्ट्वा (absolutive of √यज्) leads into the result-clause.

9.20.title: स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते — the wrong telos
9.20.body: प्रार्थयन्ते (लट् ātmanepada of प्र-√अर्थ्) governs स्वर्गतिम् — they pray for heaven-attainment. आसाद्य (absolutive of आ-√सद्) and अश्नन्ति (लट्) describe the consequent enjoyment of दिव्यान् देवभोगान् — 'divine god-pleasures'. The verse is descriptive, not yet critical; 9.21 will deliver the critique.

9.21.title: क्षीणे पुण्ये — locative absolute of merit's exhaustion
9.21.body: क्षीणे पुण्ये is a सति-सप्तमी (locative absolute) — 'when merit is exhausted'. भुक्त्वा (absolutive of √भुज्) and विशन्ति (लट् of वि-√विश्) chain the sequence: having enjoyed, they re-enter मर्त्यलोकम्. The grammar makes the descent contingent, not punitive — it follows automatically from the structure of पुण्य.

9.21.title: गतागतम् कामकामाः — the loop named
9.21.body: गतागतम् is a द्वन्द्व-like compound, द्वितीया एकवचन — 'going-and-coming' as a single object. कामकामाः is a बहुव्रीहि — 'those whose desires are desires'. लभन्ते (लट् ātmanepada) is the verse's anchor: what they 'get' is the round-trip. त्रयीधर्मम् अनुप्रपन्नाः (past participle) — having clung to the three-Veda dharma alone — is what produced this.

9.22.title: अनन्याः चिन्तयन्तः — the doubled exclusivity
9.22.body: अनन्याः ('not-other-having', nominative plural) and चिन्तयन्तः (present participle of चिन्त्) together describe the devotees who think of Krishna and no one else. पर्युपासते (लट् of परि-उप-√आस्) means 'sit around in worship' — encircling presence. The relative pronoun ये binds them to the तेषाम् of the second pāda.

9.22.title: योगक्षेमं वहामि अहम् — the famous undertaking
9.22.body: योगक्षेमम् is a द्वन्द्व compound — 'acquisition-and-preservation' — द्वितीया एकवचन object of वहामि (लट् of √वह्, 'I carry'). नित्याभियुक्तानाम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन ('of the ever-yoked'). The case-grammar is decisive: Krishna is the bearer, the devotees are the genitive beneficiaries; agency rests entirely with Him.

9.23.title: अन्यदेवताभक्ताः — the other-god-devotees
9.23.body: अन्यदेवताभक्ताः is a long तत्पुरुष — 'devotees of other deities'. श्रद्धया अन्विताः ('endowed with faith', तृतीया + ppp) concedes the sincerity of their worship. यजन्ते (लट् ātmanepada of √यज्) keeps their action active and present. The api is generous: 'even those'.

9.23.title: अविधिपूर्वकम् — the procedural shortfall
9.23.body: अविधिपूर्वकम् is an adverbial accusative — 'in a manner not preceded by proper method'. They worship 'Me alone' (माम् एव, द्वितीया with emphatic एव) but without the prescribed विधि. The verse is unusual: it affirms reference while flagging methodological deficit, preparing 9.24's diagnosis.

9.24.title: भोक्ता च प्रभुः च — enjoyer and lord
9.24.body: भोक्ता (nominative agent-noun of √भुज्) and प्रभुः together name two roles Krishna holds for सर्वयज्ञानाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन, 'of all sacrifices'). The षष्ठी is dependent on both nouns — He is enjoyer-of and lord-of every sacrifice. The hi is asseverative: 'for indeed'.

9.24.title: तत्त्वेन — the instrumental that diagnoses
9.24.body: अभिजानन्ति is लट् of अभि-√ज्ञा, 'recognise', negated by न तु. तत्त्वेन ('in truth', तृतीया एकवचन) is the missing mode of recognition — they worship without recognising-as-the-thing-itself. अतः च्यवन्ति ('therefore they fall') — च्यवन्ति is लट् of √च्यु, and the अतः supplies the causal link to the methodological failure of 9.23.

9.25.title: Four destinations from four व्रत-types
9.25.body: देवव्रताः → देवान्, पितृव्रताः → पितॄन्, भूतेज्याः → भूतानि, मद्याजिनः → माम् — the verse is a perfect four-fold parallel, each with यान्ति (लट् of √या) governing a द्वितीया-object. The taddhita -व्रत and the agent-noun -याजिन् name each devotee by the deity served.

9.25.title: अपि माम् — the inclusion in the final pāda
9.25.body: मद्याजिनः अपि माम् — 'My-sacrificers, also, (go to) Me.' The अपि is structurally striking: in a verse cataloguing how each worshipper reaches their chosen object, Krishna's devotees are added 'also' — yet their goal is the unmediated Krishna Himself. The destination matches the व्रत by exact case-symmetry.

9.26.title: Four neuter accusatives — leaf, flower, fruit, water
9.26.body: पत्रम् पुष्पम् फलम् तोयम् — four द्वितीया एकवचन objects of प्रयच्छति (लट् of प्र-√यम्, 'offers'). The very simplicity of the list is the verse's argument: the offering's worth is in its mode, not its expense. यः ... तत् is the relative-correlative scaffold.

9.26.title: भक्त्युपहृतम् — the participle that qualifies the offering
9.26.body: भक्त्युपहृतम् ('brought-forth by devotion') is a past passive participle agreeing with the implicit object. अश्नामि (लट् of √अश्, 'I eat/accept') is उत्तम-पुरुष एकवचन — the divine first-person consumption. प्रयतात्मनः is षष्ठी एकवचन ('of the one whose self is prepared/purified') — the offerer's state, not the offering's price, is the qualifying condition.

9.27.title: यत् ... तत् — five relatives, one injunction
9.27.body: यत् करोषि, यत् अश्नासि, यत् जुहोषि, ददासि यत्, यत् तपस्यसि — five relative clauses with five second-person singular लट् verbs covering action, eating, sacrificing, giving, austerity. The single तत् कुरुष्व (लोट् of √कृ) gathers them all into one imperative.

9.27.title: मदर्पणम् — the predicate of the imperative
9.27.body: मदर्पणम् ('My-offering') is द्वितीया एकवचन, predicate-accusative of कुरुष्व: 'do that as offering-to-Me'. The compound makes Krishna the dative-recipient encoded inside the accusative noun. The pedagogy is radical economy — no new act is required, only a reframing of every act.

9.28.title: शुभाशुभफलैः कर्मबन्धनैः — the dual instrumental
9.28.body: शुभ-अशुभ-फलैः and कर्मबन्धनैः are two तृतीया plural compounds joined in apposition: 'from the bonds of karma that have good-and-bad fruits'. मोक्ष्यसे (लृट् of √मुच्) is future passive — 'you shall be released' — addressed to Arjuna directly.

9.28.title: सन्न्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा — the qualifying बहुव्रीहि
9.28.body: सन्न्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा ('whose self is yoked to the yoga of renunciation') is a long बहुव्रीहि in prathama, attributively qualifying the subject of मोक्ष्यसे. विमुक्तः (ppp) modifies the same subject; उपैष्यसि (लृट् of उप-√इ) gives the destination — 'you shall come to Me'. The verse compresses 9.27's practice into one future-tense result.

9.29.title: समः — the predicate adjective of equanimity
9.29.body: समः अहं सर्वभूतेषु — समः (nominative) is the predicate, सर्वभूतेषु the सप्तमी ('toward all beings') specifying its scope. द्वेष्यः and प्रियः, both nominative predicate adjectives, are explicitly denied: न मे ... अस्ति. The षष्ठी मे ('to Me') is a dative-of-relation.

9.29.title: मयि ते तेषु च अहम् — the chiasmus of mutual indwelling
9.29.body: ये भजन्ति is relative; मयि (सप्तमी 'in Me') ते — 'they are in Me' — and reciprocally तेषु (सप्तमी 'in them') अहम् — 'I am in them'. The case-grammar makes the indwelling perfectly symmetric. The conjunction तु ('but') marks this as the exception that breaks the सम-stance just claimed.

9.30.title: सुदुराचारः अपि चेत् — the conditional concession
9.30.body: सुदुराचारः ('of very bad conduct') is a बहुव्रीहि nominative; अपि चेत् opens a maximally generous conditional. भजते (लट् ātmanepada of √भज्) governs मां, qualified by अनन्यभाक् — 'one whose share is not-elsewhere'. The grammar refuses to make the bhakta's past biography the deciding fact.

9.30.title: मन्तव्यः — the gerundive of social judgment
9.30.body: मन्तव्यः is a कृत्य form of √मन् — 'is to be regarded'. साधुः एव सः (predicate nominative with एव) is what he is-to-be-regarded as. The hi-clause gives the reason: सम्यग् व्यवसितः ('rightly resolved'). The verse is grammatically a social-instruction sentence — how the community must read this devotee.

9.31.title: क्षिप्रं भवति — the speed of transformation
9.31.body: क्षिप्रम् (adverb, 'swiftly') modifies भवति (लट् of √भू). धर्मात्मा is the predicate nominative — 'becomes one whose self is dharma'. शश्वत्शान्तिम् ('eternal peace', द्वितीया) is the object of निगच्छति (लट् of नि-√गम्). The same subject is given two verbs, ordered by tempo: quick → eternal.

9.31.title: प्रतिजानीहि — the imperative of vow-taking
9.31.body: प्रतिजानीहि is लोट् मध्यम-पुरुष of प्रति-√ज्ञा — 'declare/vow it'. Krishna asks Arjuna to make the vow himself: न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति. The double mātrā of vow (Krishna's own statement, Arjuna's required declaration) is the rhetorical point: the assurance is given through the devotee's own utterance.

9.32.title: व्यपाश्रित्य — the absolutive that levels access
9.32.body: व्यपाश्रित्य is a क्त्वान्त absolutive of वि-अप-आ-√श्रि — 'having taken complete refuge in'. The optional verb स्युः (विधिलिङ् of √अस्) introduces hypothetical inclusion: 'even those who might be पापयोनयः'. The grammar deliberately stacks distancing adverbs ('even', 'might', 'also') to widen the gate.

9.32.title: स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि — the social enumeration
9.32.body: Three nominative plural categories — स्त्रियः, वैश्याः, शूद्राः — are listed with तथा and closed by ते अपि (correlative to the earlier ये). यान्ति परां गतिम् — लट् of √या with द्वितीया object — assigns them the same supreme goal. The case-grammar is uniform across categories, which is the verse's social claim.

9.33.title: किं पुनः — the a fortiori particle
9.33.body: किं पुनः ('how much more so?') stages an argument from the lesser to the greater. ब्राह्मणाः पुण्याः and राजर्षयः stand in nominative as the implied beneficiaries; the verb is elided ('shall surely attain', from 9.32's यान्ति). भक्ताः is predicate-attributive, the operative qualifier.

9.33.title: अनित्यम् असुखम् लोकम् — three accusatives on one noun
9.33.body: अनित्यम् ('impermanent'), असुखम् ('joyless'), and इमम् ('this') all agree with लोकम् in द्वितीया एकवचन — a triple विशेषण cluster diagnosing the world. प्राप्य (absolutive of प्र-√आप्) and भजस्व (लोट् of √भज्) chain the injunction: having attained this very world, worship Me. The imperative falls on Arjuna directly.

9.34.title: Four predicate-injunctions in one pāda
9.34.body: मन्मनाः, मद्भक्तः, मद्याजी, मत्परायणः — four बहुव्रीहि compounds with मत्- as the prefixed reference. भव (लोट् of √भू) governs the first three; नमस्कुरु (लोट् of नमस्-√कृ) provides the fourth verb with मां as द्वितीया. The chapter compresses to a single fourfold imperative.

9.34.title: एष्यसि युक्त्वा — future yoked to absolutive
9.34.body: एष्यसि is लृट् of √इ ('you shall come') with माम् as object and the emphatic एव. युक्त्वा is an absolutive of √युज् ('having yoked'), governing आत्मानम्. Evam ('thus') refers back to the four mat- injunctions. The grammar binds the future-promise tightly to the act of self-yoking — the closing personal injunction is procedurally explicit.
